game_name: Double Dragon III: The Sacred Stones

design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through worldwide stages defeating enemy waves using martial arts combat, acquire power-ups and character swaps via item shops, cooperate with up to 2 other players
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (credits for items) vs combat execution; player skill vs enemy attrition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control iconic martial arts twins on a globe-trotting quest for mystical artifacts; experiment with multiple fighting styles and character types
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early microtransaction system where players spend additional credits for moves, weapons, and character roster rather than earning through play; this creates a pay-for-progression model unusual for beat-em-ups of the era. The game is also notably outsourced (to East Technology), diverging from series conventions with simplified move sets compared to predecessors.

difficulty.lives_system: Traditional lives replaced by character roster system; players can hold up to 3 extra fighter types in reserve. When current character dies, next fighter automatically selected.
difficulty.continues_system: Item shop at stage start allows purchasing 'Extra Guys' to add character reserves by spending credits
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with item shop available at start of certain stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via item shop: players can purchase health restoration, power-ups, special moves, and weapons to customize difficulty. Japanese version is notably easier (enemies deal 1/3 less damage) and removes shops in favor of character select, reducing pay-for-progression tension.

progression.upgrade_system: Credit-based item purchases: 'Energy' (health restoration to 150%), 'Power Up' (attack speed boost), 'Tricks' (unlock hurricane kick and jumping throw), 'Weapons' (stage-dependent: nunchaku in Mission 2, sword in Missions 3/5), 'Extra Guys' (add fighter to roster)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character types (Urquidez, Chin, Ōyama brothers) available stage-by-stage; Urquidez in Missions 1&5, Chin in Mission 2, Ōyama in Mission 3. Special moves unlock via 'Tricks' purchase.
replayability_hooks: Three distinct character types with different martial arts styles (mixed martial arts, tai chi, karate)
Multiplayer cooperation with up to 3 players enabling co-op strategies
Stage-specific character availability encourages replaying with different rosters
Japanese version's easier difficulty and removed shops offer alternative experience

world_building_techniques: Mysterious fortune teller named Hiruko sends protagonists on worldwide quest
Plot hook: retrieve three Rosetta Stones in Egypt
Global stage progression implies world travel narrative

faction_indicators: Fighting sibling teams representing different martial arts traditions (Lee brothers vs Urquidez/Chin/Ōyama)
Character selection creates team identity in multiplayer
danger_signals: Microtransaction economy creates pay-to-win dynamic in arcade cabinet
Currency (credits) required for character roster expansion and move unlocks
environmental_storytelling: Worldwide stage locations suggest international quest narrative
Stage-specific weapon availability (nunchaku, sword) implies environmental context

sources_used: Wikipedia - Double Dragon 3: The Rosetta Stone
